name: Stroke (Ischaemic)
anchor_event: Symptom onset (last known well time) or, if wake-up stroke, time of symptom discovery
anchor_mimic: Earliest documented NIHSS assessment or nursing note indicating stroke symptoms
total_typical_duration: Acute phase: 0-72h; subacute: 72h-14 days; early chronic: 14 days+
references: AHA/ASA 2026 Guideline for Early Management of AIS (Prabhakaran et al.)
Tissue plasminogen activator for acute ischemic stroke (NINDS rt-PA Stroke Study Group, NEJM 1995)

phases.name: Hyperacute Phase (Ischaemic Penumbra)
phases.description: Core infarct surrounded by ischaemic penumbra (salvageable tissue). Neurological deficits maximal or progressing. Treatment decisions are time-critical: thrombolysis eligibility, EVT triage. Every minute of delay = further penumbral loss.

trajectories.clinical_significance: AF detection critical for aetiology and secondary prevention
Hyperglycaemia worsens penumbral injury; must rule out hypoglycaemia (stroke mimic)

trajectories.direction: may be reduced in large territory stroke
trajectories.clinical_significance: Low GCS with LVO → urgent EVT consideration

reference_only_trajectories.expected_range: variable by territory; MCA LVO typically 10-25
reference_only_trajectories.rate: may worsen in first hours (progressing stroke)
reference_only_trajectories.clinical_significance: NIHSS drives treatment decisions: any disabling deficit → tPA eligible (AHA/ASA 2026)
reference_only_trajectories.execution_notes: Requires unsupported imaging/research assay or non-MIMIC-standard measurement.

reference_only_trajectories.clinical_significance: Must lower to <185/110 before tPA; permissive hypertension if no tPA
reference_only_trajectories.execution_notes: No safe executable mapping was found.

reference_only_trajectories.expected_range: INR ≤1.7 and platelets ≥100K required for tPA
reference_only_trajectories.execution_notes: No safe executable mapping was found.
No safe executable mapping was found.

phases.name: Early Acute Phase (Post-treatment / First 24h)
phases.description: Post-thrombolysis or post-EVT monitoring period. Neurological status may improve (reperfusion benefit) or worsen (haemorrhagic transformation). Strict BP control. Serial NIHSS monitoring. EVT still possible up to 24h for selected patients (DAWN/DEFUSE-3 criteria).

trajectories.clinical_significance: Hyperthermia worsens infarct expansion; active cooling if >38°C

trajectories.direction: should be managed but NOT tightly
trajectories.expected_range: 140-180 mg/dL (AHA/ASA 2026: Class III harm for 80-130 target)
trajectories.rate: stable with insulin drip if needed
trajectories.clinical_significance: Both hypo- and hyperglycaemia worsen outcomes; moderate control preferred

reference_only_trajectories.direction: improving if successful reperfusion; stable or worsening if not
reference_only_trajectories.rate: rapid improvement in first 2h post-tPA (dramatic responders); gradual over 24h
reference_only_trajectories.clinical_significance: Early neurological improvement is strongest predictor of good outcome
reference_only_trajectories.execution_notes: Requires unsupported imaging/research assay or non-MIMIC-standard measurement.

reference_only_trajectories.clinical_significance: BP excursions above target increase haemorrhagic transformation risk
reference_only_trajectories.execution_notes: No safe executable mapping was found.

reference_only_trajectories.expected_range: any elevation suggests concurrent cardiac injury
reference_only_trajectories.clinical_significance: Cardiac source workup; concurrent MI or takotsubo
reference_only_trajectories.execution_notes: No safe executable mapping was found.

reference_only_trajectories.expected_range: assess for haemorrhagic transformation, oedema evolution
reference_only_trajectories.clinical_significance: Symptomatic ICH occurs in ~6% post-tPA; routine CT at 24h
reference_only_trajectories.execution_notes: No safe executable mapping was found.

phases.name: Oedema / Complication Phase
phases.description: Cerebral oedema peaks. Risk of midline shift, herniation in large territory infarcts (malignant MCA syndrome). Neurological deterioration may occur despite initial improvement. Dysphagia screening, DVT prophylaxis, early mobilisation begin.

trajectories.expected_range: drop of ≥2 points from post-treatment baseline
trajectories.clinical_significance: GCS decline + pupil asymmetry = herniation → neurosurgical emergency
Post-stroke fever within 72h associated with worse outcomes; treat aggressively

trajectories.expected_range: dysphagia present in 37-78% of acute stroke
trajectories.clinical_significance: Failed swallow screen → NPO, NG feeding; aspiration pneumonia prevention

reference_only_trajectories.clinical_significance: Secondary deterioration = urgent re-imaging; consider decompressive craniectomy if large MCA infarct and age <60
reference_only_trajectories.execution_notes: Requires unsupported imaging/research assay or non-MIMIC-standard measurement.

reference_only_trajectories.clinical_significance: Cushing triad (hypertension + bradycardia + irregular breathing) = imminent herniation
reference_only_trajectories.execution_notes: No safe executable mapping was found.

reference_only_trajectories.clinical_significance: Post-stroke inflammatory response; also check for aspiration pneumonia
reference_only_trajectories.execution_notes: No safe executable mapping was found.

phases.name: Stabilisation / Early Rehabilitation Phase
phases.description: Oedema resolving. Neurological status stabilising or gradually improving. Secondary prevention initiated (antiplatelet/anticoagulant, statin, antihypertensive). Early rehabilitation and mobilisation. Risk of recurrent stroke highest in first 2 weeks.

trajectories.expected_range: target <140/90 (or <130/80 if tolerated) for secondary prevention
trajectories.clinical_significance: Long-term BP control reduces recurrence risk by ~30%

reference_only_trajectories.clinical_significance: Rate of NIHSS improvement predicts functional outcome (mRS at 90 days)
reference_only_trajectories.execution_notes: Requires unsupported imaging/research assay or non-MIMIC-standard measurement.

reference_only_trajectories.clinical_significance: Diabetes management part of secondary prevention
reference_only_trajectories.execution_notes: Requires unsupported imaging/research assay or non-MIMIC-standard measurement.

reference_only_trajectories.expected_range: ≥24h telemetry minimum; consider extended monitoring if cryptogenic
reference_only_trajectories.clinical_significance: AF detection rate increases with monitoring duration; anticoagulation if AF found
reference_only_trajectories.execution_notes: No safe executable mapping was found.

reference_only_trajectories.clinical_significance: Persistently elevated D-dimer → investigate for DVT (immobility risk)
reference_only_trajectories.execution_notes: No safe executable mapping was found.

atypical_variants.name: Wake-up stroke / Unknown onset time
atypical_variants.deviation: Onset time unknown (patient found with symptoms). Cannot apply standard time windows. DWI-FLAIR mismatch on MRI used to estimate onset window.
atypical_variants.clinical_implication: May still be eligible for tPA if DWI-FLAIR mismatch present (WAKE-UP trial); EVT if perfusion mismatch
atypical_variants.cres_task: Temporal grounding: model must recognise that treatment eligibility requires imaging-based time estimation, not clock time alone

atypical_variants.name: Minor stroke with early neurological deterioration (END)
atypical_variants.deviation: Initial NIHSS low (2-4), then sudden worsening at 12-48h. May reflect clot propagation, re-occlusion, or oedema.
atypical_variants.clinical_implication: DAPT (aspirin + clopidogrel) within 24h reduces END risk (CHANCE/POINT trials)
atypical_variants.cres_task: Trend reasoning: model must flag unexpected NIHSS worsening from a low baseline

atypical_variants.name: Haemorrhagic transformation post-thrombolysis
atypical_variants.deviation: After initial improvement, sudden NIHSS worsening with headache, vomiting, BP spike. CT shows new haemorrhage.
atypical_variants.clinical_implication: Stop tPA infusion; reverse with cryoprecipitate/TXA; neurosurgical consult
atypical_variants.cres_task: Contrastive: model must distinguish oedema-related worsening from haemorrhagic worsening using vital sign and lab patterns

atypical_variants.name: Posterior circulation stroke (basilar artery)
atypical_variants.deviation: Atypical symptom profile: vertigo, diplopia, ataxia, bilateral weakness, bulbar symptoms. NIHSS underestimates severity (NIHSS skewed toward anterior circulation). GCS may drop rapidly.
atypical_variants.clinical_implication: NIHSS ≥10 with basilar occlusion → EVT within 24h (AHA/ASA 2026 Class 1)
atypical_variants.cres_task: Diagnostic consistency: model must recognise NIHSS underestimation in posterior circulation and use additional signals

atypical_variants.name: Stroke mimic (hypoglycaemia, seizure, migraine)
atypical_variants.deviation: Focal neurological deficit resolves with glucose correction (hypoglycaemia), time (migraine aura), or is post-ictal. No infarct on imaging.
atypical_variants.clinical_implication: Glucose must be checked before tPA (AHA/ASA 2026: mandatory). tPA in stroke mimics has low bleeding risk but should be avoided.
atypical_variants.cres_task: Threshold detection: model must identify glucose <60 mg/dL as alternative explanation before attributing deficit to stroke

atypical_variants.name: Malignant MCA infarction
atypical_variants.deviation: Massive oedema with midline shift >5mm by 24-48h. NIHSS >20 with rapid deterioration despite treatment. GCS drops below 8.
atypical_variants.clinical_implication: Decompressive craniectomy within 48h reduces mortality from ~80% to ~30% in patients <60 years
atypical_variants.cres_task: Temporal reasoning: model must predict trajectory toward malignant oedema from early NIHSS, imaging, and clinical signs
